arrivalSteps.title: Complete Visit Japan Web / Arrival Card
arrivalSteps.description: Before or on the flight, register on Visit Japan Web (vjw-lp.digital.go.jp) to pre-register your immigration and customs info, or fill out the paper Disembarkation Card for Foreigner provided on board. Provide name, passport number, flight number, intended address in Japan, and purpose of visit.

arrivalSteps.title: Quarantine / Health Screening
arrivalSteps.description: Proceed through the health/quarantine checkpoint after deplaning. COVID-era requirements have been lifted as of 2024. Declare any restricted items (fresh produce, meat) on your customs form.

arrivalSteps.title: Immigration (Passport Control)
arrivalSteps.description: Join the foreigner queue at Immigration Control. Present your passport, disembarkation card (or Visit Japan Web QR code), and any required visa. All visitors aged 16+ must provide fingerprint scans and a facial photograph.

arrivalSteps.title: Baggage Claim
arrivalSteps.description: Collect your checked baggage from the designated carousel. Keep your baggage claim tags as officers may check them against bags before you exit the hall.

arrivalSteps.title: Customs Declaration
arrivalSteps.description: Submit the Customs Declaration form (one per family). Declare cash exceeding JPY 1,000,000, gifts over JPY 200,000 duty-free value, and any restricted goods. The Visit Japan Web QR code can be used for the electronic declaration lane.

arrivalSteps.title: Plant / Animal Quarantine (if applicable)
arrivalSteps.description: If carrying fresh fruit, vegetables, meat, or live animals, present them at the Quarantine counter before the customs exit. Undeclared prohibited agricultural items can result in fines or deportation.

fallbackAlerts.message: A slight bow (15°) is appropriate when greeting immigration and customs officials.
Queues are strictly observed. Do not skip ahead — it is considered deeply rude.
Failure to declare food, drugs, or large amounts of cash at customs is a criminal offence.

phrases.nativeScript: 入国審査はどこですか？
phrases.romanized: Nyūkoku shinsa wa doko desu ka?

phrases.nativeScript: 申告するものはありません。

phrases.english: Excuse me, could you help me?
phrases.nativeScript: すみません、手伝っていただけますか？

scenarios.description: Sacred sites, dress codes, and ritual etiquette

arrivalSteps.title: Remove Shoes if Required
arrivalSteps.description: Look for a sign or a rack of shoes. If present, remove yours before entering the inner hall.

arrivalSteps.title: Purify at the Temizuya
arrivalSteps.description: At the water basin, rinse left hand, right hand, then mouth (do not drink). Use the ladle.

arrivalSteps.title: Bow at the Torii Gate
arrivalSteps.description: Bow once before passing through the torii gate as a sign of respect.

fallbackAlerts.message: Remove shoes before entering inner halls. A rack at the entrance signals this is required.
Photography inside main halls is usually prohibited. Look for no-camera signs.
Speak quietly and avoid loud behaviour — these are active places of worship.

phrases.nativeScript: ここで写真を撮っていいですか？
phrases.romanized: Koko de shashin wo totte ii desu ka?

phrases.english: Where should I remove my shoes?
phrases.nativeScript: 靴はどこで脱げばいいですか？
phrases.romanized: Kutsu wa doko de nugeba ii desu ka?

arrivalSteps.title: Do Not Open the Door
arrivalSteps.description: Taxi doors in Japan open and close automatically. Never touch the door handle.

arrivalSteps.title: Show the Address in Japanese
arrivalSteps.description: Most drivers do not speak English. Show a printed or phone address in Japanese characters.

arrivalSteps.title: No Eating or Calls in Trains
arrivalSteps.description: Eating and loud phone calls are strongly discouraged on trains and buses.

fallbackAlerts.message: Taxi doors open and close automatically — never touch the handle or you may cause damage.
Phone calls on trains and buses are frowned upon. Set your phone to silent mode.

phrases.english: Please take me to this address.
phrases.nativeScript: この住所まで連れて行ってください。
phrases.romanized: Kono jūsho made tsurete itte kudasai.

phrases.nativeScript: ここで止めてください。

scenarios.description: Dining customs, tipping culture, and table etiquette

arrivalSteps.title: Do Not Tip
arrivalSteps.description: Tipping is not customary in Japan and may be refused or cause embarrassment.

arrivalSteps.title: Say 'Itadakimasu' Before Eating
arrivalSteps.description: This phrase (いただきます) expresses gratitude before a meal. It is polite to say it.

fallbackAlerts.message: Do NOT tip in Japan — it is considered rude and may be returned to you.
Slurping noodles is perfectly acceptable and even complimentary to the chef.

phrases.nativeScript: 二人用のテーブルをお願いします。
phrases.romanized: Futari-yō no tēburu wo onegai shimasu.

phrases.nativeScript: お会計をお願いします。

arrivalSteps.title: Biometric Registration
arrivalSteps.description: All visitors must provide fingerprints and a photo at immigration. This is mandatory.

arrivalSteps.title: Declare Medications
arrivalSteps.description: Some medications common abroad (e.g. pseudoephedrine, certain ADHD drugs) are illegal in Japan. Declare them.

fallbackAlerts.message: Several common medications are controlled substances in Japan. Undeclared prescription drugs can result in arrest.
Biometric fingerprinting is mandatory for all foreign visitors entering Japan.

phrases.nativeScript: 観光のために来ました。
